commit.message:
fix(ci): Add response count verification to E2E database setup (#999)

* fix(ci): Add response count verification to E2E database setup

The 71 E2E test failures are caused by tests waiting for
[data-testid="response-item"] which never appears when responses
aren't seeded. Enhanced verification to:

- Check response count in addition to user count
- Check topic count for completeness
- Fail fast if any critical data is missing
- Show db-seed logs for debugging

This helps identify seeding failures before tests run.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(ci): Query postgres directly for E2E database verification

The previous approach used `docker exec` into discussion-service to run
Prisma queries, but that failed because:
- discussion-service may not be fully initialized when verification runs
- Prisma db execute command has complex output parsing

Now queries postgres container directly with psql:
- psql is native to postgres container, always available
- postgres is guaranteed ready since db-seed completed
- -tAc flag gives clean numeric output

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: steiner385 <steiner385@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add missing test dependencies to devDependencies (#28)

vitest, @testing-library/react, @testing-library/user-event,
@testing-library/jest-dom, jsdom, react, react-dom, @mui/material,
and react-router-dom were all used directly but only available as
transitive dependencies through @kinvolk/headlamp-plugin. pnpm's
strict module resolution prevented them from being resolved.

Also adds process.env.NODE_ENV="test" to vitest config so React
loads its development build (required for act() support in tests).

Fixes #27

Comprehensive product, UX, and feature expansion for Regulate

Phase 1 — Polyvagal repositioning:
- NSStateSelector component with 4 nervous system states
- SOSFlow rewritten with polyvagal-branched paths (hyperactivated,
  activated, window of tolerance, hypoactivated)
- Bilateral tapping with Web Audio stereo panning
- Extended exhale breathing pattern

Phase 2 — UX/Design maturity pass:
- Replaced all emojis with custom SVG icons (Icons.tsx)
- Standardized completion screens across all modules
- Typography, copy, and interaction refinements
- One-handed use improvements (larger tap targets)

Phase 3 — Product & feature expansion (10 items):
- Unmissable SOS button with slow pulse animation
- Scenario-specific SOS flows (home/public/work/night)
- Soothed vs move-through-it approach choice
- Post-session aftercare flow with journal auto-logging
- Apple Health hook architecture (useHealthLog)
- Voice guidance architecture (useAudioGuide)
- Trust statement and crisis resources page
- Session timing with "ready to continue" prompts
- Learn section reframe with soft intro
- Onboarding copy rewrite

Phase 4 — Engagement, personalization, and polish (15 items):
- Streak tracker on home screen
- Unified favorites system with star toggles
- Session history page with pattern detection
- Adaptive recommendations based on aftercare data
- Custom breathing patterns with duration sliders
- Expanded affirmations (3 new categories, personal tab)
- Guided journaling prompts (contextual by trigger/technique)
- SOS scenario-specific instructions per context
- Grounding variations (sensory, body, object)
- Sleep page with 4-7-8 breathing + muscle relaxation
- Haptic feedback library with named patterns
- Accessibility (skip-to-content, aria-live, reduced motion, high contrast)
- Page transition animations
- Onboarding recall (review/edit preferences)
- PWA install prompt

New files: Icons.tsx, NSStateSelector.tsx, SOSFlow rewrite,
AftercareFlow.tsx, BinauralBeats.tsx, MyPerson.tsx, OnboardingGate.tsx,
crisis/page, caregiver/page, safety-plan/page, onboarding/page,
settings/page + preferences, history/page, sleep/page, template.tsx,
useWakeLock, useHealthLog, useAudioGuide, haptics, streak, favorites,
recommendations, journal-prompts, sos-scenarios libs

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Batch hook events into single IPC message per flush (#719)

## Summary
- Hook events previously used queue mode (`merge: false`), causing each
accumulated event to be sent as a separate IPC call on flush — 20 events
× N windows = 20N IPC messages
- Switched to merge mode with a `mergeFn` that collects events into an
array, so all accumulated events within the 50ms window are sent as a
single IPC message per window
- The preload layer transparently unpacks batched arrays, so all
renderer consumers are unaffected

Fixes #649

## Changes
- **`src/main/util/ipc-broadcast-policies.ts`**: Changed hook event
policy from `merge: false` (queue mode) to `merge: true` with a custom
`mergeFn` that accumulates events into an array. Single events remain
unwrapped for backwards compatibility; multiple events are batched as
`[event1, event2, ...]`.
- **`src/preload/index.ts`**: Updated `onHookEvent` listener to handle
both single events and batched arrays, dispatching each event
individually to the callback.
- **`src/main/util/ipc-broadcast-policies.test.ts`**: Updated test to
verify batched output (1 IPC call with array) instead of individual
calls. Added test for single-event case remaining unwrapped.

## Test Plan
- [x] `ipc-broadcast-policies.test.ts` — verifies multiple hook events
are batched into a single IPC message with array payload
- [x] `ipc-broadcast-policies.test.ts` — verifies a single hook event is
sent unwrapped (backwards compatible)
- [x] `ipc-broadcast.test.ts` — all 21 existing broadcast tests continue
to pass (queue mode still works for other consumers)
- [x] TypeScript type checking passes (pre-existing picomatch error
only)
- [x] Lint passes on all changed files

## Manual Validation
1. Open the app with multiple windows (main + popout)
2. Run an agent that triggers rapid hook events (e.g. multiple tool
calls in succession)
3. Verify hook events still appear correctly in all windows
4. Confirm in DevTools Network/Performance that IPC messages are reduced
during bursts

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ci: add E2E preflight check for Headlamp connectivity and plugin version (#24)

Adds a diagnostic step before E2E tests that:
- Logs the expected plugin version from package.json
- Verifies Headlamp is reachable (fails fast if not)
- Attempts to list installed plugins for debugging

This surfaces version mismatches and connectivity issues immediately
instead of requiring analysis of cryptic test timeout failures.

Fix maps (Leaflet self-contained geocoding) + activate test data migrations

Map.tsx: Complete rewrite — now fully self-contained with Leaflet + OpenStreetMap.
Uses Nominatim for address geocoding (no API key needed). New API:
  - searchAddress prop triggers internal geocode + map fly-to
  - onPinDrop fires when user clicks map
  - markerPosition prop controls the pin
  - No more onMapReady with Google Maps dependency

ProjectsPage: Remove all direct Google Maps API calls (Geocoder,
AdvancedMarkerElement). Replace with searchAddress + onPinDrop + onGeocodeResult
props on MapView. Address "Find" button now works via Nominatim.

eliminates the squash-merge conflicts that occur when dev and master
diverge after squashing.

feat: Advanced visualisation overhaul — 14 new components, turquoise theme, SVG map markers

Sprint 1-3 of the visualisation upgrade plan:

New reusable components (14 files):
- ChoroplethMap: Ward-level heat map with multi-stop color scales
- SparkLine: Inline SVG mini chart for tables/cards
- GaugeChart: Radial arc gauge for scores
- TreemapChart: Hierarchical area chart wrapper
- WaterfallChart: Cumulative breakdown (income/expense)
- HeatmapGrid: GitHub-contribution-style day/week grid
- BumpChart: Ranking changes visualisation
- ChartTooltip: Enhanced rich tooltip with trends
- ChartGradient: Parametric SVG gradient for charts
- ChartGradients: Preset gradient definitions
- MapIcons: SVG marker icon factory (7 icon types)
- MapLegend: Interactive map legend panel
- AdvancedCharts.css: All new component styles

Page upgrades (12 pages):
- Demographics: Choropleth tab with metric selector
- DOGE: GaugeCharts (fraud triangle), TreemapChart, HeatmapGrid
- Spending: TreemapChart, HeatmapGrid calendar, SparkLines
- Budgets: WaterfallChart, stacked area, GaugeChart
- Elections: SparkLines in ward table, Brush zoom, animations
- Integrity: GaugeChart, PieChart risk distribution, SparkLines
- CrossCouncil: RadarChart comparison, BumpChart rankings, Brush
- Constituencies: Expense BarChart, PieChart, HeatmapGrid voting
- Meetings: Monthly BarChart, type PieChart, calendar HeatmapGrid
- MyArea: SparkLine cards, GaugeChart, ethnicity PieChart
- PropertyPortfolio: EPC PieChart, TreemapChart, valuation BarChart
- Highways: SVG icon markers replacing circles, selected glow

Turquoise accent (#00d4aa) replaces blue (#0a84ff):
- 41 CSS files + 36 JSX files updated
- Nav, logo, sidebar, all page accents
- Ward boundaries, legends, focus states

Global chart enhancements:
- CHART_ANIMATION (800ms ease-out) on all Bar/Line/Area/Pie
- Brush zoom/pan on 8+ time-series charts
- Gradient fills on Area charts

Gzip compression fix:
- Filter to JS/CSS/HTML/SVG only (was compressing 500MB+ data files)
- Build time: minutes → 14.5s

105 files changed, 4829 insertions, 825 deletions.

commit.message:
fix: correct package name from solaris to polaris (#26)

* ci: add E2E preflight check for Headlamp connectivity and plugin version

Adds a diagnostic step before E2E tests that:
- Logs the expected plugin version from package.json
- Verifies Headlamp is reachable (fails fast if not)
- Attempts to list installed plugins for debugging

This surfaces version mismatches and connectivity issues immediately
instead of requiring analysis of cryptic test timeout failures.

Fix avatar arms, voice, persona, and memory reliability

  don't stick out straight.

  match WOW's character.
- Persona: Rewrite WOW from "Celestial Artisan" with mystical speech
  to "Creative Partner" — casual, direct, enthusiastic. No more
  celestial metaphors or poetic mysticism.
- Memory: Add retry with backoff for embedding API calls. Intermittent
  rate limits (429) now retry once after 500ms instead of immediately
  failing. Raised circuit breaker threshold from 3 to 5.

Fix receipt images not showing in main app

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix node labels hidden by z-ordering, stop auto-opening Endpoints tab

Root cause of disappearing labels: SVG z-ordering. When nodes are drawn
sequentially, later nodes' circles cover earlier nodes' text elements
because SVG paints in DOM order. Node 10's text sits below node 11's
circle in the DOM tree.

Fix: After drawing all nodes, MOVE (not clone) all .node-text and
.tag-text elements into a labels layer <g> appended at the end of the
SVG. This ensures all text renders above all circles. On mobile, set
pointer-events:none so touches pass through text to circles below.
On desktop, pointer-events remain active for Ctrl+drag label repositioning.

Previous clone-based approach failed because invisible originals
(opacity:0) still had pointer-events:auto, blocking touch targets.
Moving (not cloning) eliminates this — no invisible originals remain.

Also: removed auto-opening of Endpoints tab when Route Finder opens
on mobile — user wants just the tab strip without content panel.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
feat: add reader connection status tracking across stack (#148)

## Summary

- Add independent `reader_connected` status tracking to distinguish
forwarder↔reader TCP connections from forwarder↔server WebSocket
connectivity
- Surface reader connection state on server HTTP API, SSE events, and
receiver
- Show reader disconnection warnings on receiver UI when forwarder loses
reader connection

## Implementation

**Protocol:** New `ReaderStatusUpdate` (forwarder→server) and
`ReaderStatusChanged` (server→receiver) WsMessage variants

**Server:** Persist `reader_connected` column in streams table, handle
status updates from forwarder, fan out to receivers and dashboard via
SSE

**Forwarder:** Send reader status updates on TCP state transitions and
initial burst after hello

**Receiver:** Handle status changes and emit UI warnings on reader
disconnect

**Dashboard:** Display reader connection state alongside online status

## Test Coverage

- 2 serde round-trip tests for new protocol types
- 1 E2E integration test verifying status flow from forwarder through
server to HTTP API
- 276+ existing tests continue to pass across all packages

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
